name: Security Check
description: Run intelligent security checks using cdk-nag and/or ASH based on context. Analyzes results and provides actionable remediation guidance.

then.prompt:
The user has requested a security check. Follow this workflow:

1. DETERMINE SCOPE:
   - If user specified files/directories, scan only those
   - If user mentioned 'CDK', 'infrastructure', or 'CloudFormation', focus on cdk-nag
   - If user mentioned 'Lambda', 'Python', 'code', 'secrets', or 'dependencies', focus on ASH
   - If user said 'full' or 'comprehensive', run both tools
   - Default: Run both cdk-nag and ASH on entire codebase

2. SELECT TOOLS AND COMMANDS:
   
   A. cdk-nag (CDK/CloudFormation infrastructure security):
      - cdk-nag runs automatically during CDK synthesis via Aspects
      - Command: cd backend && npx cdk synth 2>&1
      - Output: Annotations appear in synthesis output
      - Look for: Lines with [Error] or [Warning] containing rule IDs (e.g., AwsSolutions-IAM4)
      - Format: [Error at /StackName/ResourceName] AwsSolutions-XXX: Description
      - Note: cdk-nag must be installed (npm install cdk-nag) and integrated in backend/bin/app.ts
   
   B. ASH (Comprehensive code, secrets, dependencies, IaC scanning):
      - ASH is a CLI tool that orchestrates multiple scanners
      - Installation check: uvx --version (if fails, install: curl -sSfL https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh)
      - Command format: uvx git+https://github.com/awslabs/automated-security-helper.git@v3.1.12 [options]
      
      - Execution modes:
        * Local (fast, Python-only): --mode local
        * Container (comprehensive, requires Docker): --mode container
        * Precommit (fastest subset): --mode precommit
      
      - Key options:
        * --source-dir <path>: Directory to scan (default: current directory)
        * --output-dir <path>: Where to store results (default: .ash/ash_output)
        * --scanners <list>: Specific scanners to run (comma-separated)
        * --exclude-scanners <list>: Scanners to skip
        * --fail-on-findings: Exit with error if findings detected
        * --output-formats <list>: Output formats (default: text,flat-json,sarif,html,markdown)
      
      - Example commands:
        * Full scan: uvx git+https://github.com/awslabs/automated-security-helper.git@v3.1.12 --mode local
        * Specific directory: uvx git+https://github.com/awslabs/automated-security-helper.git@v3.1.12 --mode local --source-dir backend/lambda
        * Container mode: uvx git+https://github.com/awslabs/automated-security-helper.git@v3.1.12 --mode container
      
      - Output files (in .ash/ash_output/):
        * ash_aggregated_results.json: Complete machine-readable results
        * reports/ash.html: Interactive HTML report
        * reports/ash.summary.txt: Human-readable text summary
        * reports/ash.summary.md: Markdown summary
        * reports/ash.sarif: SARIF format for CI/CD

3. RUN SELECTED TOOLS:
   - Execute appropriate commands based on scope
   - For ASH: Use full uvx command with git URL (don't assume 'ash' alias exists)
   - Capture full output for analysis
   - If command fails, explain why and suggest fixes
   - Check if tools are installed before running

4. PARSE RESULTS:
   
   For cdk-nag findings:
   - Extract from synthesis output lines matching pattern: [Error|Warning at /Path] AwsSolutions-XXX: Description
   - Components: Severity (Error=High, Warning=Medium), Resource path, Rule ID, Description
   - Group by resource for clarity
   - Note: cdk-nag findings appear in CDK synthesis output, not separate files
   
   For ASH findings:
   - Primary source: Read .ash/ash_output/ash_aggregated_results.json
   - JSON structure includes: scanner, severity, rule_id, file, line, message, suppressed
   - Also check reports/ash.summary.txt for human-readable summary
   - Group by severity (Critical > High > Medium > Low)
   - Identify actionable vs suppressed findings
   - Note which scanners ran: bandit, semgrep, detect-secrets, checkov, cfn-nag, etc.

5. PRIORITIZE FINDINGS:
   - Critical/High severity first
   - Secrets/credentials exposure (immediate risk)
   - IAM overpermissions (wildcards, admin access)
   - Data encryption issues (S3, DynamoDB, in-transit)
   - Dependency vulnerabilities with known exploits
   - Medium severity in production code
   - Low severity and informational

6. PROVIDE REMEDIATION:
   For each finding:
   - Explain the security risk in plain language
   - Show the problematic code/configuration with file path and line number
   - Provide a concrete fix with code example
   - For cdk-nag: Show how to fix in CDK code OR how to suppress with NagSuppressions
   - For ASH: Show code fix OR suppression comment (e.g., # nosec B201)
   - Indicate if suppression is appropriate (test data, intentional design, framework limitation)
   - Reference relevant CIC standards from security-practices.md steering file
   - Use ADR format for documenting suppressions

7. OFFER TO FIX:
   - Ask if user wants you to implement fixes
   - Prioritize fixes by severity
   - Apply fixes using appropriate tools (editCode, strReplace)
   - For cdk-nag suppressions: Add NagSuppressions.addResourceSuppressions() calls
   - For ASH suppressions: Add inline comments with justification
   - Document all suppressions with ADR format in code comments

8. SUMMARY:
   - Total findings by severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
   - Breakdown by tool (cdk-nag vs ASH scanners)
   - Number of findings fixed vs documented vs suppressed
   - Key security issues that need immediate attention
   - Recommendations for preventing similar issues
   - Suggest adding ASH to CI/CD if not already present

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- ASH command: Always use full uvx command with git URL, not 'ash' alias
- ASH installation: Check uvx availability first, provide installation command if missing
- cdk-nag: Runs during synthesis, not as separate command
- cdk-nag integration: Must be added to backend/bin/app.ts using Aspects.of(app).add(new AwsSolutionsChecks())
- Parse tool output carefully - don't hallucinate findings
- Read actual output files (ash_aggregated_results.json, synthesis output)
- Reference security-practices.md steering file for CIC security standards
- Document suppressions in code comments with ADR format
- For Windows: ASH commands work in PowerShell/CMD, use appropriate path separators
